verdict.justification: Assessed statically, without a runtime pass. One high-severity authorization gap (SEC-01) must be fixed before opening signups beyond the invite list. No critical findings. Reliability and accessibility issues are real but not launch-blocking for an invite-gated audience.

shipGates.note: Responsive layout with no fixed-width core paths; touch targets meet 44px. Device-level checks are runtime (see Not assessed).
A static pass cannot confirm the activation funnel fires end to end. Run the instrumentation discipline against a live session to verify one identity across client and server.

findings.issue: Server action updates a household row using a client-supplied householdId without checking the caller's membership.
findings.impact: Any authenticated user can rename another household by invoking the action with a foreign householdId.
findings.evidence: lib/settings/actions.ts:42 reads householdId from FormData and passes it to the update at line 55; no membership lookup between. RLS policy on households (migrations/0008) covers select but the update policy uses USING (true).
findings.recommendedFix: Resolve householdId from the caller's membership server-side instead of trusting the form value, and tighten the update policy to membership-scoped USING/WITH CHECK.

verification.refutationNotes: Skeptic checked middleware, layout gate, and all migrations touching households; no compensating control found. rls-isolation tests do not cover update.

findings.issue: Submit button stays enabled while the server action is in flight; double-click creates duplicate list items.
findings.impact: Duplicate shopping items under normal double-click behavior; no data corruption.
findings.evidence: No pending state from useActionState is applied to the button's disabled prop (components/shopping/add-item-form.tsx:31); the insert has no idempotency key.
findings.recommendedFix: Disable the control while pending and add a client-generated idempotency key or a unique constraint on (list_id, normalized_name).
findings.assumption: Assumes no debouncing exists in a parent component; none was found in the component tree read.

verification.refutationNotes: Originally high; skeptic confirmed duplicates are possible but showed deletes are one tap and no financial or shared-state damage occurs, failing the high bar.

findings.issue: Modal renders without a focus trap; Tab moves focus to content behind the overlay.
findings.impact: Keyboard and screen-reader users can interact with obscured content while the modal is open.
findings.evidence: components/ui/modal.tsx:12 renders a fixed overlay div with no focus management; no inert or aria-modal usage anywhere in the file.
findings.recommendedFix: Add aria-modal, role=dialog, and a focus trap (or use the native dialog element); restore focus to the trigger on close.
findings.reproduction: Open any modal, press Tab repeatedly, observe focus outline on background controls.

remediationPlan.title: Membership-scope all household mutations
remediationPlan.detail: Fix the two actions and the RLS update policy together, then extend rls-isolation tests to cover updates.

quickWins.title: Disable submit while pending in add-item-form
quickWins.blastRadius: One form component; worst case a stuck disabled state on error, mitigated by useActionState reset.

deeperInvestigation.title: Idempotency strategy for all inserts
deeperInvestigation.detail: Duplicate-on-retry applies beyond shopping items; needs a project-wide convention (unique constraints or idempotency keys).

notAssessed.check: Focus visibility and contrast on rendered UI
notAssessed.howToVerify: Run /production-audit --runtime, or tab through the app with axe DevTools open at 200% zoom.
